expectations.evidence: Found multiple times in response.md (e.g. line 33 import, line 76 `CurationV1.insert_curation(s)`, line 80 `part_name="CurationV1"`, TL;DR step 1).
Appears throughout the response, e.g. line 14 `spyglass.spikesorting.spikesorting_merge.SpikeSortingOutput`, line 36 import, line 80 `SpikeSortingOutput.insert([cur_key], part_name="CurationV1")`.
Found at line 16, line 45, line 117 (`### 1d. Bundle into a `UnitWaveformFeaturesGroup``), line 124 (`UnitWaveformFeaturesGroup().create_group(...)`), and TL;DR step 5.
Multiple instances: line 16, line 46, line 213 section heading `## 4. Tie it together: ClusterlessDecodingSelection`, line 232 `ClusterlessDecodingSelection.insert1(...)`.
Found at line 17, line 48 import, lines 154-167 (section `### 2b. Bundle into a `PositionGroup``), TL;DR step 7.

expectations.text: Identifies `CurationV1` as the mandatory next step (initial curation, not optional) before anything downstream of spike sorting can be reached
expectations.evidence: Section 1a states: `UnitWaveformFeaturesSelection is keyed off SpikeSortingOutput's merge_id, not off SpikeSorting directly. So each sorting_id needs: 1) an "initial" curation row in CurationV1 (this is the no-op curation #0 you create right after sorting)`, with code `CurationV1.insert_curation(s)`. TL;DR puts `CurationV1.insert_curation(...)` as step 1. The 'Common gotchas' section reinforces: `The selection table requires the spikes side to be promoted into SpikeSortingOutput; it does not read from SpikeSorting/CurationV1 directly.` This communicates curation 0 as the mandatory first step.

expectations.text: Calls out the merge insert into `SpikeSortingOutput` (e.g. `insert([curation_key], part_name='CurationV1')`) as a separate explicit step that does not happen automatically
expectations.evidence: Line 73-81 shows the two-step pattern explicitly with comments `# 1) seed a curation row` and `# 2) push that curation into the merge table`, with code `SpikeSortingOutput.insert([cur_key], part_name="CurationV1")`. TL;DR lists step 2 as a separate item: `2. SpikeSortingOutput.insert([...], part_name="CurationV1")`. Reinforced in Common gotchas: 'The selection table requires the spikes side to be promoted into SpikeSortingOutput; it does not read from SpikeSorting/CurationV1 directly.'

claims.evidence: Confirmed in spyglass source: spyglass/decoding/v1/waveform_features.py defines UnitWaveformFeaturesSelection with `-> SpikeSortingOutput.proj(spikesorting_merge_id='merge_id')` projection. The response correctly notes this gotcha in code comment line 104 and Common gotchas line 282.
Matches spyglass/decoding/v1/core.py DecodingParameters.insert_default which inserts these four with version-suffixed names from non_local_detector.
Confirmed in spyglass/decoding/v1/core.py PositionGroup.create_group signature includes `position_variables` and `upsample_rate` params.

claims.claim: Encoding and decoding intervals must not overlap if `estimate_decoding_params=True`
claims.evidence: Spyglass clusterless.py make() raises if intervals overlap when estimate_decoding_params is True; this is a documented constraint.

claims.claim: ClusterlessDecodingV1 has fetch_results() and fetch_model() helpers
claims.evidence: Inherited from DecodingOutputMixin / DecodingMixin in spyglass.decoding.decoding_merge / v1.clusterless.

claims.claim: Clusterless decoding ignores curation labels and uses all spikes from the units in the merge entry
claims.evidence: UnitWaveformFeatures.make extracts features from all units in the SortingAnalyzer regardless of label; clusterless does not filter by accept/mua/noise. This is consistent with spyglass docs and source.

suggestions.reason: Substring assertions for 5 of the 7 checks are easy to satisfy and a hand-wavy answer that name-drops the tables without explaining the workflow would still pass them. The two semantic assertions (mandatory curation, explicit merge insert) carry most of the discriminating power.

No assertion checks that the response surfaces the `spikesorting_merge_id` vs `merge_id` rename, which is the most common silent failure when constructing UnitWaveformFeaturesSelection inserts. The response did call this out, but a less careful answer that just used `merge_id` would still pass all current expectations.

No assertion checks that `UnitWaveformFeaturesGroup` and `PositionGroup` are described as manual tables requiring an explicit `create_group(...)` call. A response could mention the table names without explaining this gating step and still pass.

eval_feedback.overall: Two semantic assertions (mandatory curation, separate merge insert) do real work; the five substring assertions are easy filler. Consider adding semantic assertions about the `spikesorting_merge_id` rename and the manual `create_group()` step on the two Group tables.
